narration: Here's what science actually says about stroke types.

narration: Strokes come mainly in two forms: ischemic and hemorrhagic. But what does that really mean?
visual_description: Context visual: Strokes come mainly in two forms: ischemic and hemorrhagic. But what does that really mean?
ai_image_prompt: Context visual: Strokes come mainly in two forms: ischemic and hemorrhagic. But what does that really mean?
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Context visual: Strokes come mainly in two forms: ischemic and hemorrhagic. But what does that really mean?

narration: Ischemic strokes occur when blood clots block blood flow to the brain.
visual_description: Escalation visual: Ischemic strokes occur when blood clots block blood flow to the brain.
ai_image_prompt: Escalation visual: Ischemic strokes occur when blood clots block blood flow to the brain.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Escalation visual: Ischemic strokes occur when blood clots block blood flow to the brain.

narration: On the other hand, hemorrhagic strokes happen when a blood vessel bursts, leaking blood into the brain.
visual_description: Evidence visual: On the other hand, hemorrhagic strokes happen when a blood vessel bursts, leaking blood into the brain.
ai_image_prompt: Evidence visual: On the other hand, hemorrhagic strokes happen when a blood vessel bursts, leaking blood into the brain.
ai_video_prompt: Cinematic motion: Evidence visual: On the other hand, hemorrhagic strokes happen when a blood vessel bursts, leaking blood into the brain.

narration: Did you know that symptoms can vary? Sudden weakness, confusion, and trouble speaking are key signs!
visual_description: Pattern Interrupt visual: Did you know that symptoms can vary? Sudden weakness, confusion, and trouble speaking are key signs!
